name: London Ambulance Service
description:
The London Ambulance Service dispatches ambulances in emergencies. 
    The key goal is to allocate an available ambulance for every call that can reach the scene within 11 minutes. 
    The entire dispatch process must not exceed a set maximum time limit.

    To make this work, the computer system (CAD) analyzes incident forms and assigns vehicles. 
    It is vital to track the exact location of moving ambulances. 
    This requires staff to follow standard routes and correctly communicate departure and destination, 
    allowing radio operators and the CAD to record data accurately.
highLevelGoals: The dispatcher aims to allocate an available ambulance for every emergency call promptly, ensuring that the response time does not exceed 11 minutes.
The ambulance driver seeks to respond to emergency calls quickly and efficiently, adhering to the set maximum time limit for dispatch.
The radio operator aims to maintain clear and accurate communication with ambulance drivers to ensure smooth coordination during dispatch operations.
The CAD system aims to analyze incident forms effectively to assign the most suitable vehicles for emergency calls in a timely manner.
The emergency caller seeks a reliable and prompt response from the ambulance service when calling for assistance during emergencies.
lowLevelGoals: The dispatcher requests the current status of ambulances to determine which are available for dispatch.
The dispatcher selects an ambulance from the available list and assigns it to the incoming emergency call.
The dispatcher changes the status of the ambulance in the system to reflect that it is on its way to the emergency.
The ambulance driver checks the system for live tracking of their route to the emergency location.
The ambulance driver notifies the system that they have arrived at the emergency site.
The ambulance driver uses the communication system to inform the dispatcher of any problems that may affect response time.
The radio operator uses the communication system to relay information and updates to the ambulance drivers.
The radio operator records all messages exchanged with ambulance drivers in the system.
The CAD system processes incoming incident reports to match them with the appropriate ambulance resources.
The CAD system compiles data on response times to assess efficiency and identify areas for improvement.
The emergency caller uses the system to connect with the ambulance service for immediate help.
The emergency caller communicates essential details regarding the emergency to ensure appropriate response.
